---
title: "AI Literacy in Education"
---

![](static/logo_3.png)

## Welcome

This website grew out of a conversation series on AI Literacy in Education held at St. Olaf College. 
It is designed to help people at other institutions start the same conversation and to give you the tools to do it well.

## Objectives

The goal of this series was simple: to bring students and faculty together for an honest, structured conversation about generative AI tools: the role they currently play and the role(s) they could/should/shouldn’t play in teaching and learning. 

## Who Should Use This Website

This website is for anyone who wants to hold similar conversations at their own institution, 
whether you are a faculty member, an administrator, a student organizer, or anyone else trying to 
move your community toward a more thoughtful, shared understanding of GenAI in education. You do not need to replicate the series exactly. Use as much or as little as is useful for your context.

## How to Use This Website

In an ideal world, you would move through the materials in roughly this order:

[**Start with the survey**](survey/index.qmd) — Before anything else, use the survey to take stock of where your community is. It will help you understand the attitudes and experiences people are bringing into the room so you can design conversations that meet them there. 

The survey is broken down into three sections:

- [**Attitudes towards GenAI Use**](survey/attitudes.qmd) — Explores respondents' current stance on GenAI tools, with a focus on the academic context.

- [**Current Uses of GenAI**](survey/current_uses.qmd) — Captures how respondents currently engage with GenAI tools in relation to their academic work, whether in teaching or learning.

- [**Readiness to Use GenAI Tools**](survey/readiness.qmd) — Invites respondents to self-assess how prepared they feel to incorporate GenAI tools into their teaching and learning practice.

**Work through the Discussion Sessions in order.** Each session builds on the last:

- [**Session 1**](Bookclub/discussion1.qmd) helps your community understand where it currently stands and where it might be 
heading. 

- [**Session 2**](Bookclub/discussion2.qmd) moves into deeper territory: ethics, trust, and transparency. Rather than jumping 
to rules, it asks the prior question: *What values should be guiding us?*

- [**Session 3**](Bookclub/discussion3.qmd) translates those values into action. It is a practical session focused on developing 
concrete guidelines for how individual faculty members or perhaps your institution at large can approach GenAI in teaching and learning.

[**Read about how it went at St. Olaf.**](Bookclub/stolaf_relections.qmd) If you want a sense of what these conversations look like 
In practice — what came up, what surprised us, what we would do differently — you can read about how the series unfolded in our own community.

[**Explore the Resources tab.**](about/resources.qmd) Whether you are new to GenAI or looking to go deeper, the Resources tab hosts a curated list of free materials to help you and your community better understand and use these tools.

## A note on GenAI use 

This website was built with the assistance of generative AI tools. Specifically, I used Claude AI (Sonnet 4.6 Adaptive) to help with formatting content in Quarto Markdown. 

All ideas, arguments, written content, and decisions reflected here are my own. GenAI tools were not used for the generation of any written content of any kind. 


![](static/logo_1.png)
